close_reason: Set local_models.mode: shadow in routing.yaml. Commit 00c285a in Clavain repo.
description: Set local_models.mode: shadow in os/Clavain/config/routing.yaml so Clavain logs which tasks would route to local inference without actually routing them. This is the safe first step before enforce mode.
title: [interfere] Enable Track B5 shadow mode in Clavain routing.yaml

description: Clavain's 4-track routing is more intelligent than LiteLLM (task-aware complexity, evidence calibration, safety floors) but lacks operational capabilities: (1) cost tracking/attribution, (2) cloud provider fallback with retries, (3) rate limit management, (4) unified API gateway, (5) usage observability, (6) privacy-aware routing. With interfere providing local serving, Clavain needs these to route between local and cloud without a proxy.
title: Competitive Landscape: Close Clavain routing gaps vs LiteLLM/OpenRouter

description: Create interlab campaigns for each experiment: early exit (entropy-based layer skipping), reservoir routing (frozen-layer MLP), and any other planned techniques. Define baselines, success metrics, and kill conditions per AGENTS.md experiment specs.
title: [interfere] Start interlab experiment campaigns for esoteric techniques

close_reason: GitHub repo created: https://github.com/mistakeknot/interfere (public). Initial commit with 38 files, 2905 lines. Branch protection: configure via dashboard.
description: Run gh repo create mistakeknot/interfere (public), push initial code, and add the repo to clone-subrepos.sh so it's included in Demarch ecosystem cloning.
title: [interfere] Create GitHub repo and add to clone-subrepos.sh

description: Run the downloaded Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-4bit through interfere's server. Measure: tok/s, time-to-first-token, quality on standard prompts (coding, reasoning, tool calling). This is the smallest MoE in the Pareto frontier — 35B total, 3B active, ~20GB, should fit entirely in RAM.
title: [interfere] Benchmark Qwen3.5-35B-A3B 4-bit via mlx-lm

description: Build flash-moe, extract weights from the downloaded Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-4bit, and benchmark. Measure: tok/s at 4-bit, expert cache hit rate, per-layer timing. Compare to flash-moe's M3 Max results (4.36 tok/s). Estimate: 7-10 tok/s on our M5 Max 128GB.
title: [interfere] Benchmark Qwen3.5-397B-A17B via flash-moe SSD streaming

close_reason: Feasibility study complete. M5 Max SSD benchmarks: 36 GB/s seq, 27 GB/s random pread (1.5-2x faster than M3 Max test rig). Estimated 7-10 tok/s for 397B model at 4-bit. Recommend subprocess bridge integration. Doc: docs/investigations/2026-03-26-flash-moe-feasibility.md
description: Research integrating flash-moe (github.com/danveloper/flash-moe) into the interfere pipeline. flash-moe is a pure C/Metal engine that runs Qwen3.5-397B-A17B by streaming expert weights from SSD. On a 48GB M3 Max it achieves 4.36 tok/s at 4-bit. On our 128GB M5 Max, expert caching would be dramatically better (most active experts stay in RAM), potentially 8-12+ tok/s. Integration options: (1) subprocess bridge calling flash-moe binary, (2) port SSD-streaming techniques into our MLX pipeline, (3) hybrid where flash-moe handles C3+ tasks and mlx-lm handles C1-C2. This would be a game-changer for sovereignty — running a 397B model locally. Should be an interlab experiment campaign.
title: [interfere] Investigate flash-moe integration for 397B MoE inference

close_reason: Server runnable via 'uv run python -m server [--dry-run]'. InferenceEngine verified with mlx-community/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct-4bit. 18/18 tests pass. Changes local only — no git repo yet (Demarch-26p).
description: Download a working model (qwen3:30b via ollama or equivalent MLX model) and verify it serves completions via the /v1/chat/completions endpoint. This validates the inference path end-to-end.
title: [interfere] Pull and serve a model (ollama or MLX)

description: Run the downloaded Qwen3.5-122B-A10B-4bit through interfere's server. Measure: tok/s, time-to-first-token, quality on standard prompts. 122B total, 10B active, ~65GB, fits in RAM on 128GB M5 Max.
title: [interfere] Benchmark Qwen3.5-122B-A10B 4-bit via mlx-lm

description: Once downloaded (~449GB), benchmark Kimi K2.5-3bit. 1T total params, 32B active, 384 experts K=8. Largest model in the frontier. Will require SSD streaming engine. Quality at 3-bit quantization needs verification — may need 4-bit if quality degrades.
title: [interfere] Benchmark Kimi K2.5 3-bit (1T, 32B active)

description: Once downloaded (~419GB), benchmark GLM-5-4bit. GLM-5 benchmarks competitively with Claude Opus 4.5 (77.8 SWE-bench, 61.1 Terminal-Bench). 744B total, 40B active, uses DeepSeek Sparse Attention. Will require SSD streaming engine. This could be the best local model for coding tasks.
title: [interfere] Benchmark GLM-5 4-bit (744B, 40B active)

description: Parent epic for interfere initial bringup. Enable shadow routing in Clavain, serve a model via ollama/MLX, wire Metal worker to InferenceEngine, start interlab experiment campaigns, and create the GitHub repo. These are the first concrete steps to make interfere operational.
title: [interfere] Phase 1: Bring up local inference pipeline

close_reason: Metal worker wired to InferenceEngine. Full pipeline: HTTP → Metal subprocess → mlx-lm stream_generate → SSE. Verified e2e with Qwen2.5-0.5B. Commit 188e350.
description: Connect the Metal subprocess worker (server/metal_worker.py) to actually call InferenceEngine (server/inference.py). Currently the queue-to-inference plumbing is incomplete — the worker receives requests but doesn't execute inference.
title: [interfere] Wire Metal worker to InferenceEngine

close_reason: Updated routing.yaml tier_mappings and complexity_routing to MoE-first models (Qwen3.5-35B-A3B, Nemotron-Cascade-2-30B-A3B, gpt-oss-120b). Updated AGENTS.md memory budget. Clavain commit 145a83b, interfere commit 0e9e57b.
description: Replace the current complexity-to-model mapping in routing.yaml and AGENTS.md with newer MoE models that are significantly faster and better quality. Current: qwen3-8b/qwen3-30b/qwen2.5-72b. Proposed: Qwen3.5-9B-OptiQ-4bit (C1 draft), Qwen3.5-35B-A3B 4-bit (C2, MoE 3B active), Nemotron-Cascade-2-30B-A3B 8-bit or gpt-oss-120b MXFP4 (C3). MoE models give big-model quality at small-model speed because only a fraction of params are active per token. Also update the memory budget in AGENTS.md to reflect actual model sizes.
title: [interfere] Update model tier routing to MoE-first models

description: After all benchmarks complete, compile results into a Pareto frontier analysis. Plot tok/s vs quality (SWE-bench proxy, coding eval, tool-calling success). Determine optimal routing: which model for which complexity tier. Update routing.yaml with empirically validated tiers. This is the capstone deliverable for the model evaluation campaign.
title: [interfere] Publish Pareto frontier analysis: speed vs quality across local models

description: Once downloaded (~378GB), benchmark DeepSeek V3.2-4bit. Will require SSD streaming engine (flash-moe port or mlx-lm with offloading). 672B total, ~37B active. Measure tok/s and quality vs Qwen3.5 family.
title: [interfere] Benchmark DeepSeek V3.2 4-bit (672B, ~37B active)
